description: 77 Stoke Newington Church St was used as a social housing/bedsit, while the use class remained C3(dwellinghouse). This application seeks planning permission and listed building consent for internal alterations to sensitively restore the property's historic character while restoring it for sustainable, modern single family use. The works focus on reinstating the original plan form by removing later partitions where sensible, restoring historic features such as fireplaces, cornicing, shutter boxes, and floorboards, and introducing traditionally designed joinery and fittings throughout. Non-original elements, including built-in cupboards and outdated kitchens and bathrooms, will be removed or reconfigured. New kitchens, bathrooms, and storage will be introduced in discreet locations using existing service routes where possible, minimising disruption to the building fabric. A new ensuite and utility area will be incorporated on the upper floors to support contemporary living needs. All existing windows, non-historic and dating from mid-to-late 20th century, will be refurbished or replaced with timber slimline double-glazed units, improving energy performance while respecting the building's appearance. Historic staircases and flooring will be repaired and restored, with sympathetic new finishes applied throughout.
